subject: French

preamble.description: The French syllabus is drawn from the Senior High School curricular of member countries and seeks to impact students with the requisite knowledge, skills, attitudes and values to communicate effectively in French for personal development and understanding of and participation in the affairs of the Sub-region and the world at large.

general_aims_and_objectives: This syllabus is intended to test the candidates' ability to use the four language skills namely, listening, speaking, reading and writing to promote communicative competence.

papers.description: Will consist of forty multiple choice questions, to be answered within 1 hour for 40 marks.

Will consist of two sections with three essay questions each. Candidates will be required to answer two questions in all, one question from each section. Both questions will be answered within 1 hour 15 minutes for 40 marks.
Will test candidates' listening comprehension, and reading and conversation abilities. Each candidate will require 40 minutes for the test. The paper will carry 50 marks.

scheme_of_examination.note: Papers 1 and 2 will be a composite paper, to be taken at one sitting.

paper_1.section_a_comprehension: Designed to test candidates ability to read and understand standard written documents in French. Two passages, each of about 150 words, will be provided and a total of five short questions will be set on each. Multiple choice questions only; candidates required to answer all questions.
paper_1.section_b_structure: Designed to test candidates' ability to use, in appropriate communicative contexts, French words, expressions and structural patterns. Thirty short questions will be set and candidates will be required to attempt all of them.

paper_2.description: Made up of two sections: (a) Essay and (b) Letter writing. It will test candidates' ability to write freely and correctly on topics of general interest, using the appropriate structures, registers and tenses.
paper_2.section_a_essay: Three questions will be set. Candidates are expected to answer one question only. Expected length about 100 words.
paper_2.section_b_letter_writing: Three questions will be set. Candidates are expected to answer one question only. Expected length about 100 words.

paper_3.listening_comprehension: Will test candidates' ability to listen to, and understand a straightforward passage in French about 200 words long based on issues of general interest. Read twice during the first 10 minutes. Candidates study questions for 3 minutes before second reading, then 7 minutes to answer 10 questions in writing (may include open-ended questions, transformation, and substitution).
paper_3.reading: Aims to test candidates' ability to read aloud, with appropriate pronunciation and fluency. A simple passage of about 100 words of contemporary interest read once at a normal pace.

conversation.duration: About 10 minutes per candidate based on themes of general and contemporary interests (sports, health, leisure, education, politics, culture, etc.).
conversation.dialogue: Test candidates' ability to express themselves freely in a dialogue by responding to a minimum of ten questions (for Ghana, five questions will be on selected literature texts).
conversation.exposition: Test candidates' ability to speak freely on a given topic in French, making a minimum of 5 sentences.

notes_on_the_syllabus_structure.parts_of_speech: Articles (definite, indefinite, partitive - gender and number); Verbs (regular/irregular, common moods/tenses, standard expressions like avoir + noun, venir de + infinitive); Nouns (complements, gender, number); Pronouns (personal, possessive, indefinite, demonstrative, relative, impersonal); Adjectives (possessive, demonstrative, indefinite, interrogative - agreement and position); Adverbs (formation and position, special adverbs like mal, vite, fort, tout); Negatives (ne...pas, ne...rien, ne...jamais, etc.); Conjunctions (et, mais, donc, ou, parce que, etc.); Prepositions (à, de, sur, en, devant, etc.).
notes_on_the_syllabus_structure.sentence_patterns_and_expressions: Declarative, interrogative, exclamative; Voice (active/passive); Direct/reported speech; Compound and complex sentences; Idiomatic expressions & proverbs; Forms of writing (formal/informal); Context and meaning (politeness, disgust, disappointment); Common figures of speech (similes, metaphors, hyperboles); Structures of major 'Acte de Parole' (exprimer la déception, la probation, le dégoût, la surprise, le plaisir, le regret, etc.).
